page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor includes 105 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. System memory consists of 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2045.0 and single-threaded gzip compression throughput of 33.24 GB/sec. Multi-core capabilities are characterized by a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 16105.87 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12124.0. Memory performance shows a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1332.05 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 97.49 GB/sec and 74.81 GB/sec, respectively. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, support specialized workloads, as demonstrated by an LLM text generation speed of 289.46 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 14.59 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

The c7i-flex.4xlarge offers a cost-effective alternative within the compute-optimized tier by utilizing a flexible resource allocation model that balances compute power and memory density without the premium cost of fully dedicated, non-flex instances. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, web serving, moderate database operations, and light machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores), 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2045.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.24 GB/sec. Its processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), enabling efficient execution of mathematical and cryptographic operations. Positioned as a resource-dense and cost-efficient option within the compute-optimized class, the c7i-flex.4xlarge is designed for workloads such as web serving, batch processing, software compilation, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for cost-efficient batch processing and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A cost-efficient, compute-optimized virtual instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 32 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
Strong single-core performance and text processing capabilities
No local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for web serving, batch processing, and light LLM inference
